channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Muere Sam Neill, protagonista de ‘Parque Jurásico’
item.description: A lo largo de cinco décadas de carrera, participó en más de 150 producciones, aunque quizá se le recuerde sobre todo por su papel protagonista en la saga de los dinosaurios.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:description: Sam Neill en Los Ángeles en 2024

item.title: Un búnker de Hitler podría ser demolido en un Berlín que necesita viviendas
item.description: La posible demolición del sitio pone de relieve el debate en Alemania entre proteger su patrimonio histórico y hacer frente a la crisis de vivienda.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni

media:content.media:credit: Reiner Janick, vía Berlin Underworlds Association
media:content.media:description: El búnker de la Nueva Cancillería del Reich.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for July 13, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Housing or Flood Protection? In Brooklyn, Housing Is Winning.
item.description: A towering apartment development has been approved for the waterfront, even though some residents would have preferred parkland.
item.dc:creator: Hilary Howard

media:content.media:description: This portion of the Brooklyn waterfront is a former oil and gas hub. Much of it still needs to be cleaned up.

item.title: Civics Groups Are at Odds With Mamdani Over Push for Open Primaries
item.description: If a new effort to persuade voters to change New York City’s closed primary system gains steam, a potential showdown between its backers and the mayor awaits.
item.dc:creator: Sally Goldenberg and Jeffery C. Mays

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani has defended New York’s current closed primary system, saying that recent election results have shown increased voter participation.

item.title: Sam Neill, New Zealand Screen Actor and Leading Man, Dies at 78
item.description: He appeared in more than 150 productions over a five-decade career but was perhaps best known for his star turn in the “Jurassic Park” series.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:description: Sam Neill in Los Angeles in 2024.

item.title: ‘Lucky’ and 6 More Shows to Watch on TV this Week
item.description: A new crime thriller comes to Apple TV. And Sean Evans hosts “Hot Ones: Extra Heat.”
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

media:content.media:description: Anya Taylor-Joy in “Lucky.”

item.title: The World Must Not Forget About the Genocide in Sudan
item.description: The longer the fighting drags on, the greater the chance that regional instability will spread.
item.dc:creator: The Editorial Board
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity

item.title: It’s the Scent of Manure to Most, but ‘the Smell of Money’ to Them
item.description: The Netherlands has been trying to figure out what to do with manure it can no longer use thanks to environmental rules. Volatile fertilizer costs may help.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Maciek Nabrdalik for The New York Times

item.title: Japan Is Building a New Intelligence Agency With Help From the West
item.description: Facing threats from Russia and China, Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is breaking with World War II-era limits on security.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández

media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi at the Group of 7 summit in France last month. Ms. Takaichi wants Japan to do more to protect state secrets and vital technologies and more aggressively guard against foreign influence operations.

item.title: No Corrections: July 13, 2026
item.description: No corrections appeared in print on Monday, July 13, 2026.

item.title: Why Young People in China Are Buying Feelings
item.description: Young people in China are increasingly anxious about the future and spending on feel-good experiences and products that provide “emotional value.” A companion hiker and cosplayer show what it’s like to be part of this booming consumer market.
item.dc:creator: Jiawei Wang, Jon Hazell and Rafaela Balster

item.title: Quote of the Day: A Newly Declassified Trove Underwhelms a U.F.O. Capital
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Monday, July 13, 2026.

item.title: U.A.W. President Says He’s the Victim of ‘Bogus Allegations’
item.description: A grand jury is investigating whether Shawn Fain used his position to seek favors for his fiancée and retaliated against the union official who denied them.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing, Noam Scheiber and Alan Feuer

media:content.media:description: Shawn Fain, president of the United Automobile Workers, said on Sunday that he was the victim of “bogus allegations” intended to sway upcoming union elections.

item.title: ‘House of the Dragon’ Season 3, Episode 4 Recap: Heavy Is the Head
item.description: In her attempts to establish order, Rhaenyra is already alienating some of her most vital allies, including the Sea Snake.
item.dc:creator: Sean T. Collins

media:content.media:description: Corlys (Steve Toussaint) left King’s Landing to battle pirates on the sea. It isn’t the most obvious mission for a hand of the queen to undertake.

item.title: Shooting in Southern Illinois Leaves 5 Family Members Dead
item.description: Two other people were wounded. The Illinois State Police said on Sunday that two teenage suspects were in custody.
item.dc:creator: Christina Morales and Alexandra E. Petri

item.title: What Is an Aortic Dissection?
item.description: The condition that killed Senator Lindsey Graham is deadly, sudden and difficult to treat — “like a knife to the back,” one expert said.
item.dc:creator: Gina Kolata

media:content.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Senator Lindsey Graham speaking to reporters on Capitol Hill last year. He died on Saturday.

item.title: Fire in Bangkok Bar Leaves at Least 27 Dead
item.description: The fast-moving blaze sent people fleeing toward the bathrooms to hide, officials said. Their bodies were found there, raising questions about safety standards.
item.dc:creator: Kittiphum Sringammuang, Ephrat Livni and Muktita Suhartono

media:content.media:description: At least 27 people died after the fire at Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao in the Thai capital, officials said.

item.title: It Takes Coordination (and Many Arms) to Unfurl a World Cup Flag
item.description: Teams of stagehands work to coordinate how the giant flags are brought onto the field and displayed before each match. The prep takes days.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei and Scott McIntyre

item.title: After Weeks of Silence, McConnell Says He Is Recovering From a Fall
item.description: The Republican senator denied that he had suffered a heart attack and said he had left the hospital and moved to a physical rehabilitation center. He did not give a timetable for returning to Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Diaz

media:content.media:description: Senator Mitch McConnell on Capitol Hill earlier this year.

item.title: Oil Prices Surge After Iran and U.S. Trade Strikes
item.description: The renewed hostilities posed fresh risks to ships seeking to navigate the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Jason Karaian and Lisa Friedman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Ships at the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for July 13, 2026
item.description: Lynn Lempel makes a scene.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Maddie McGarvey for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: If you’re behind the plate for Cincinnati, and the umpire sees you cheating? You’ve been caught red-handed catching Red-handed.

item.title: With Lindsey Graham Gone, Ukraine Loses an Ally in Trump’s Ear
item.description: The South Carolina Republican was Ukraine’s most influential champion inside President Trump’s mostly ”America First” political orbit.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison

media:content.media:description: Senator Graham during a news conference after a meeting with President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine in Kyiv on Friday.

item.title: Here's what we know about Lindsey Graham’s death
item.description: Senator Lindsey Graham died on Saturday night, a day after returning from Ukraine and four months before he was to face re-election.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Diaz
item.category: The Russo-Ukrainian War: The Return of History (Book)

media:content.media:credit: Will Crooks for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Senator Lindsey Graham during an appearance at the BMW manufacturing facility in Spartanburg, S.C., last month.

item.title: Venezuela’s Post-Quake Politics
item.description: People are openly and forcefully voicing their anger at the government for the first time in years.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A collapsed public housing complex in Caraballeda, Venezuela.

item.title: Mick Jagger sabe que puede que haya dado su última gira con los Rolling Stones
item.description: La legendaria estrella del rock, que ahora tiene 82 años, habla de cómo la fama, las giras y el paso del tiempo lo han cambiado.
item.dc:creator: David Marchese

media:content.media:credit: Philip Montgomery for The New York Times

item.title: In Terrifying Seconds, a Bison Charges Campers at Yellowstone
item.description: A tourist was hospitalized after a visibly agitated bull bison flipped him about eight feet in the air near a campground road.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan

media:content.media:description: A tourist taking photographs of bison in Yellowstone National Park in 2024.

item.title: States Prepare Lawsuit to Block Paramount’s Merger With Warner Bros.
item.description: State attorneys general are said to be getting ready to file a lawsuit as soon as this week to halt the $111 billion deal, a potential major obstacle.
item.dc:creator: David McCabe, Benjamin Mullin and Lauren Hirsch

media:content.media:credit: Stella Kalinina for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The acquisition, which is expected to close this year, would concentrate ownership of two major movie studios and a global news network, CNN.

item.title: El gobierno de Trump cita a periodistas del Times en una nueva ofensiva contra la prensa
item.description: El Departamento de Justicia busca obligar a declarar a reporteros que informaron sobre el nuevo Air Force One. El Times calificó la medida como un “acto descarado”.
item.dc:creator: Michael M. Grynbaum

media:content.media:description: El presidente Donald Trump hablando con los periodistas antes de subir al nuevo Air Force One la semana pasada.

item.title: Young Campers Describe Helicopter Rescue From Missouri Floodwaters
item.description: Roughly 200 campers and counselors were evacuated from Camp Taum Sauk in Black Hawk helicopters on Friday after southeastern Missouri experienced a foot of rainfall.
item.dc:creator: Alisa Shodiyev Kaff and Pooja Salhotra

item.title: Senator Lindsey Graham of South Carolina Dead at 71
item.description: Senator Lindsey Graham, a South Carolina Republican and staunch ally of President Trump, died from “a brief and sudden illness” on Saturday, his office said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper

media:content.media:credit: Will Crooks for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Senator Lindsey Graham speaking in Spartanburg, S.C., on June 30.

item.title: Michael Cohen Helped Convict Trump. Now, He’s Making Nice Again.
item.description: A previously unreported encounter last summer set the stage for a rapprochement between the president and his former fixer, who has so far avoided the diatribes and prosecutions that President Trump has directed at other critics.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman and Ben Protess

media:content.media:description: Michael Cohen outside a Manhattan courtroom in 2023. He testified in Mr. Trump’s criminal trial that he was directed to falsify records by the president, who was ultimately convicted on 34 counts.

item.title: Graham’s Death Highlights Congress’s Age Issue
item.description: By the standards of the aging Senate, where the average age is more than 65, the South Carolina Republican wasn’t particularly old.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni

media:content.media:description: From left, Senators John Cornyn, Lindsey Graham and Charles E. Grassley during a hearing last July. Mr. Grassley, at 92, is third in the line of succession to the presidency.

item.title: Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa, Ruler Who Transformed Qatar, Dies at 74
item.description: Under Sheikh Hamad, the tiny nation of Qatar developed immense natural gas wealth and cultivated influence far beyond its borders.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim

media:content.media:description: Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani is often described as the founding father of modern Qatar.

item.title: Why Dior’s Jonathan Anderson Is Inspired by Lynda Benglis
item.description: Lynda Benglis redefined sculpture in the 1960s. No longer overlooked, her audacious talent moves from museums to runways, transformed by Dior’s Jonathan Anderson.
item.dc:creator: Julia Halperin

item.title: ‘Moana’ Falters at the Box Office, Casting Doubt on Disney’s Formula
item.description: The live-action remake took in roughly $43 million over the weekend in North America, about 30 percent less than Disney had expected.
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes

media:content.media:description: Dwayne Johnson as Maui in “Moana,” which cost an estimated $250 million to make and at least $100 million to market worldwide.

item.title: Lindsey Graham Championed Israel, Even as Wars Eroded American Support
item.description: The Republican senator from South Carolina had an intense interest in the Middle East, and was a staunch supporter of U.S. strikes on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity

media:content.media:description: Senator Lindsey Graham, in 2024 in Washington, leading a Republican news conference to condemn the Biden administration’s decision to pause the delivery of bombs to Israel.

item.title: How Trump Failed to Secure the Strait of Hormuz in His Iran Deal
item.description: President Trump signed an agreement that Iran said gave it control of the waterway — and global energy supplies. Now, Iran’s military is violently asserting authority.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong, Michael Crowley and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: President Trump at Joint Base Andrews on Thursday. He recently said the memorandum of understanding reached with Iran was “over.”

item.title: Trump Recalls Last Conversation With Lindsey Graham Before His Death
item.description: In an interview on Sunday, President Trump recounted his last conversation with Senator Lindsey Graham, hours before his death. Mr. Graham sounded “perfect,” he said.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green and Olivia Diaz

media:content.media:description: President Trump with Senator Lindsey Graham during a campaign rally in Charlotte, N.C., in 2020. Mr. Trump said he spoke on the phone with Mr. Graham hours before the senator’s death.

item.title: Residents of Spanish Towns Caught in Fierce Blazes Recount Harrowing Escapes
item.description: Many say communication was chaotic and even nonexistent as flames tore through the area, taking 12 lives in one of the deadliest wildfires on record in Spain.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz, Carlos Barragán and Lynsey Chutel

item.title: This Healthy Roasted Fish Al Pastor Is Already Earning Raves
item.description: “This is absolutely delicious! Sweet, citrusy, pungent, smoky. And looks gorgeous too,” writes a reader.
item.dc:creator: Mia Leimkuhler

media:content.media:credit: Armando Rafael for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Rebecca Jurkevich.
media:content.media:description: Paola Briseño-González’s roasted fish al pastor.

item.title: Zelensky Announces Leadership Shuffle as War Turns in Ukraine’s Favor
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky said Ukraine’s prime minister, Yuliia Svyrydenko, would step down amid a broader shake-up in Ukraine’s senior leadership.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer

media:content.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Yuliia Svyrydenko, prime minister of Ukraine, at her office in Kyiv, Ukraine, in September.

item.title: Tim Scott and Other South Carolina Leaders Mourn Lindsey Graham
item.description: The senator had sometimes struggled to maintain support from his party’s most right-wing voters. But leaders and supporters paid tribute to his wit and stalwart support of South Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane and Eduardo Medina

media:content.media:description: Senator Lindsey Graham in January.

item.title: Melbourne Symphony Wins Discrimination Case Over Gaza Comments
item.description: The pianist Jayson Gillham had sued the orchestra after it canceled his performance in the wake of comments he made against Israel’s war with Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Derrick Bryson Taylor

media:content.media:description: The classical pianist Jayson Gillham outside federal court in Melbourne, Australia, in May.

item.title: Pop Stars are Confessing Their Secrets
item.description: And so are you.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Jackson

media:content.media:description: Rosalía at Madison Square Garden.

item.title: At World Cup Ticket Prices, Even Messi Can’t Fill a Stadium
item.description: The Argentine team and its superstar, Lionel Messi, have been among the biggest draws at the World Cup, but that was not enough to prevent vacant seats at its quarterfinal on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja

media:content.media:description: Lionel Messi of Argentina on the field Saturday in Kansas City during a World Cup quarterfinal match against Switzerland.

item.title: The Summeriest Skillet Pork Chops, Courtesy of Ali Slagle
item.description: And the fettuccine Alfredo my teenager makes over and over.
item.dc:creator: Margaux Laskey

media:content.media:credit: Armando Rafael for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Rebecca Jurkevich.

item.title: The Burdens and Benefits of Big Families
item.description: Readers respond to “Life Is Better With Siblings,” an Opinion guest essay by Catherine Ruth Pakaluk.

item.title: Lindsey Graham Was Facing Re-election in November. What Happens Now?
item.description: The senator’s death triggers an Aug. 11 special Republican primary election, and South Carolina’s governor can appoint a replacement to serve out the remaining term, through early January.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner

media:content.media:description: Senator Lindsey Graham at the Capitol in Washington in January.

item.title: Cold Plunging
item.description: A writer reflects on the healing powers of trying new things.
item.dc:creator: Taffy Brodesser-Akner

media:content.media:description: Senator Lindsey Graham in 2024.
